assistpool_alias_help: Human-readable key, unique within your project.
assistpool_initiator_can_assist_help: Allow the initiator to contribute to their own pool.
assistpool_active_help: Inactive configs reject new instance creation.
assistpool_no_configs: No assist-pool configs yet. Create one to get started.
apikeys_admin_keys_desc: Server-to-server API keys for admin access. Keep these secret.
apikeys_client_credentials_desc: Client credentials for C-end access with HMAC identity verification.
apikeys_delete_admin_key_desc: This will permanently delete the key. Any services using it will lose access.
apikeys_delete_credential_desc: This will permanently delete the credential. Any clients using it will lose access.
apikeys_credential_created_desc: Copy both keys now. The secret will not be shown again.
apikeys_admin_key_created_desc: Copy this key now. It will not be shown again.
checkin_day_number_hint: Which consecutive check-in day triggers this reward.
checkin_day_number_out_of_range: Day number must be between 1 and {max}.
checkin_dirty_day_warning: {count} reward(s) outside the current cycle range. Edit the day number or delete.
checkin_no_target_hint: This config has no target — rewards apply to any future day. Set a target for a structured schedule.
checkin_month_grid_hint: Day numbers are sequential (1-31), not aligned to a real calendar.
checkin_target_over_recommend: Configurations with target > 365 are hard to maintain. Consider splitting.
checkin_test_user_desc: Using your admin account as the test user
checkin_reset_progress_confirm_desc: This will permanently delete the user's check-in progress. They will start fresh on their next check-in. This action cannot be undone.
item_delete_definition_desc: This will permanently delete this item definition and all inventory data associated with it.
item_delete_category_desc: This will permanently delete this category. Item definitions using this category will become uncategorized.
item_add_valid_item: Add at least one item with a valid definition
role_write_denied_tooltip: Your role cannot perform this action
role_write_denied_toast: Your role has no write permission. Contact a project admin.
accept_invitation_sign_in_first: Please sign in or register before accepting the invitation.
accept_invitation_failed: Could not accept this invitation. Check the link or contact the inviter.
accept_invitation_success: Invitation accepted. Redirecting to the dashboard…
mail_field_target_hint: Broadcast reaches every user in the project. Multicast only reaches the listed IDs.
mail_field_recipients_hint: One per line or comma-separated. Max 5000.
mail_field_rewards_hint: Rewards are granted atomically when the user taps Claim. Empty list = announcement-only.
mail_field_require_read_hint: When on, users must open the mail before Claim becomes active.
mail_field_expires_at_hint: After this time the mail is hidden and unclaimable. Leave blank for no expiry.
mail_error_recipients_required: At least one recipient is required for multicast.
mail_revoke_confirm_desc: Users will no longer see this mail. Claim history is preserved.
mail_delete_confirm_desc: This removes the message and every user's read/claim state for it. Cannot be undone.
shop_color_hint: Hex value used in tag badges (e.g. #ef4444).
shop_reward_items_hint: Growth packs typically leave this empty — rewards live on each stage.
shop_trigger_config_hint: JSON config — e.g. itemDefinitionId + threshold for accumulated cost.
shop_delete_product_desc: This permanently removes the product. Purchase history is preserved on item logs.
shop_delete_category_desc: This removes the category. Products in it become uncategorized.
shop_delete_tag_desc: This removes the tag and unbinds it from every product.
shop_delete_stage_desc: This removes the stage definition. Existing claims are preserved.
banner_field_alias_hint: Slug used by the client app to resolve this group. Leave blank to keep it as a draft.
banner_banners_empty: No banners in this group yet.
banner_field_target_user_ids: End user IDs (comma or newline separated)
banner_delete_group_desc: This removes the group and all its banners.
dialogue_field_alias_hint: Slug used by the client app to trigger this script. Leave blank to keep it as a draft.
dialogue_nodes_empty: This script has no nodes yet. Add at least one.
dialogue_delete_script_desc: This permanently removes the script and all player progress for it.
dialogue_error_start_node_invalid: Start node must match one of the nodes
dialogue_error_duplicate_option_id: Duplicate option id within a node
dialogue_error_bad_next: 'next' points to a node that does not exist
cdkey_optional_universal_code: Leave blank to auto-generate a unique string.
collection_delete_album_desc: Are you sure you want to delete this album? This cannot be undone — all groups, entries, milestones and player progress will be wiped.
collection_field_alias_hint: Lowercase letters / digits / - _, unique within the project.
collection_delete_group_desc: Are you sure you want to delete this group? Entries under it will be moved to ungrouped.
collection_entry_description: Bind an item definition — players unlock this entry when their owned quantity reaches the threshold.
collection_delete_entry_desc: Are you sure you want to delete this entry? Players' unlock progress for it will be cleared.
collection_entry_trigger_hint: Players unlock when owned quantity reaches the threshold.
collection_milestone_description: Configure unlock rewards: by a specific entry, by collecting a group, or by collecting across the whole album.
collection_delete_milestone_desc: Are you sure you want to delete this milestone? Players who already claimed it keep their records.
collection_milestone_scope_locked: Scope cannot be changed after creation
collection_milestone_error_no_reward: Please add at least one reward item
collection_milestone_error_reward_def: Please choose an item for every reward row
collection_milestone_error_entry_require: Entry is required when scope = entry
collection_milestone_error_group_require: Group is required when scope = group
collection_rescan_hint: Scan the player's inventory and backfill missed unlocks (mail is NOT re-sent).
invite_settings_enabled_desc: When off, all bind/qualify calls for this tenant are rejected.
invite_settings_code_length_desc: Character count for new codes (no separators). Existing codes are unchanged.
invite_settings_allow_self_invite_desc: Allow inviter = invitee (off by default to prevent abuse).
invite_no_settings: No invite settings configured; using defaults (enabled / length 8 / self-invite off).
invite_delete_desc: The invitee can be bound again. Task rewards already granted will NOT be auto-rolled back.
entity_delete_schema_desc: This will permanently delete the schema and all associated blueprints, skins, and instances.
entity_delete_blueprint_desc: This will permanently delete the blueprint and all associated skins and instances.
entity_delete_skin_desc: This will permanently delete this skin.
entity_delete_formation_desc: This will permanently delete this formation config and all player formations using it.
level_delete_config_desc: Are you sure you want to delete this config? All stages, levels and player progress will be wiped.
level_delete_stage_desc: Are you sure you want to delete this stage? Levels under it will lose their stage reference.
level_delete_level_desc: Are you sure you want to delete this level? Player progress for it will be wiped.
common_discard_description: You have unsaved changes. If you close now, your edits will be lost.
task_visibility_hint: Who can see and progress this task. Broadcast = all users; Assigned = only users with an active assignment row.
task_default_ttl_hint: If an assignTask call doesn't provide expiresAt or ttlSeconds, this value is used. Leave blank for no default expiry.
task_assign_broadcast_notice: This task is configured as Broadcast, so assignments are not required for users to see it. Assignment rows are still recorded (useful for audit / personalization tags).
task_assign_new_hint: Grant access to one or more users. Existing active assignments are skipped unless 'Refresh existing' is on.
task_assign_ttl_placeholder: Override the default TTL for this batch
task_assign_ttl_hint: Overrides the definition-level default. Leave blank to fall back.
task_assign_source_ref_hint: Free-form tag for audit / tracing. Attached as source_ref on every row in this batch.
task_assign_allow_reassign: Refresh existing (update assignedAt / expiresAt on active rows)
task_assign_invalid_ttl: TTL must be a positive integer
media_asset_delete_confirm: Delete asset "{name}"? The object will be permanently removed from storage.
media_picker_description: Pick an existing asset from the drive, or upload a new one into the default folder.
media_picker_upload_hint: New assets upload to the default folder — you can reorganize them later in the media library.
common_link_activity_hint: Binding to an activity makes this entry follow the activity's cleanup policy when it archives. Leave empty for a permanent entry.
currency_delete_hint: All user wallets and ledger entries will be removed. This cannot be undone.
currency_alias_hint: Optional URL-friendly key. Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens, underscores. Unique per project.
currency_sort_hint: Lower values sort first — drives both admin list ordering and the in-game wallet display.
reward_entry_empty_hint: No entries yet. Click below to add a reward / cost.
reward_empty_hint: No rewards configured. Click "Add reward" to start.
reward_create_hint: After saving, you can configure rewards on the detail page.
reward_key_required: Please provide a valid key value
triggers_description: Visually configure Event → Condition → Action automation rules. Rules execute asynchronously via queue when events fire — typically within seconds.
triggers_empty: No trigger rules yet. Click "New rule" to configure one.
triggers_action_archive_confirm: Archive this rule? It will stop firing but historical executions are kept.
triggers_editor_name_placeholder: e.g. Cleared Lv10 → unlock map B
triggers_editor_description_placeholder: What this rule does and why.
triggers_editor_event_help: Lists only events with capability 'trigger-rule'. Opt-in missing events from the Event Catalog first.
triggers_action_stub_notice: (Stub: this action type is not yet wired to its business module — saves will fail with not_implemented)
triggers_condition_jsonlogic_help: Empty = always fires. See placeholder below for syntax.
triggers_action_unlock_feature_help: Inserts into feature_unlocks; idempotent on (org, user, key) — repeat unlocks no-op.
triggers_event_payload_hint: Reference these field names in condition / action JSON
webhooks_description: Subscribe external services to internal events. Up to 5 endpoints per project. Deliveries are signed with HMAC-SHA256 and retried automatically.
webhooks_no_endpoints: No webhook endpoints yet. Create one to start receiving events.
webhooks_create_desc: The signing secret will be shown only once after creation. Store it before closing the dialog.
webhooks_field_event_types_hint: Comma-separated. Leave blank to subscribe to everything. Use `name.*` to match any event in a namespace.
webhooks_secret_created_title: Endpoint created — copy the signing secret
webhooks_secret_created_desc: This is the only time apollokit will show this secret in plaintext. Store it in your receiver before closing.
webhooks_secret_rotated_desc: The previous secret is invalid immediately. Update your receiver before closing this dialog.
webhooks_delete_desc: All queued and historical deliveries for this endpoint will also be removed. This cannot be undone.
webhooks_rotate_desc: A new secret will be issued and shown once. The previous secret becomes invalid immediately.
webhooks_paused_warning: Auto-paused after {count} consecutive failures. Set status back to active to resume.
event_catalog_description: All business events this project can reference. Internal events are declared in code; external events are auto-discovered the first time they arrive via the client event endpoint.
event_catalog_empty: No events yet. Send an event via POST /api/client/task/events to populate this catalog.
event_catalog_read_only_hint: Internal events are declared in code and can't be edited here. Update the module's registerEvent call if the schema has changed.
event_catalog_inferred_hint: Fields shown here were auto-inferred from incoming payloads. Save to promote this to canonical and stop automatic field updates.
event_catalog_description_placeholder: Explain what this event represents and when it should fire.
event_catalog_fields_hint: Dot-paths that appear in payloads. Mark fields required once you're confident; rename or remove typos. Saving upgrades this entry to canonical.
announcement_delete_confirm_desc: This action is irreversible. Players currently viewing it will no longer see it on their next /active call.
announcement_field_alias_hint: Unique within the tenant. Lowercase letters, digits, '-', '_'; must start with a letter or digit.
announcement_field_priority_hint: Higher numbers come first. Defaults to 0.
analytics_users_coming_soon: Composition, active users, retention, and funnels — coming after the MVP.
analytics_modules_coming_soon: Switch between battle-pass, check-in, shop, tasks, leaderboards and more — coming soon.
analytics_activity_coming_soon: Full-picture view of a single activity container — funnel, point flow, sub-activity breakdown — coming soon.
analytics_logs_coming_soon: Request logs + event logs + trace correlation, powered by Tinybird — coming soon.
analytics_logs_subtitle: Event and trace queries powered by Tinybird. Left: event-type distribution over the past 30 days. Right: full event stream for a given trace_id.
analytics_logs_events_subtitle: 30-day counts grouped by event name. Source: Tinybird `tenant_event_counts`.
analytics_logs_events_empty_hint: Events appear seconds after an admin action or a player report.
analytics_logs_trace_subtitle: Paste a trace_id to see every business event emitted during that request.
analytics_logs_trace_hint: Trace IDs come from the `requestId` field in any API envelope.
analytics_logs_trace_empty_subtitle: This trace_id has no business events — it might be a pure HTTP request (only in `http_requests`), or the trace has aged out (180-day retention).
analytics_logs_fetch_failed: Query failed — please retry, or check the console for details.
analytics_explore_subtitle: Pick an event, time range, and dimension to drill into trends and breakdowns.
analytics_explore_event_empty: No events in this time range
analytics_explore_json_section_hint: Field names come from event_data top-level keys. Open the Activity page and expand a row to see what's available.
analytics_explore_json_invalid: Field name must match [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]* — top-level keys only this round.
analytics_explore_window_too_long: JSON-field queries are limited to a 30-day window.
analytics_explore_chart_empty: No matching data in this time range
analytics_explore_table_top_n_hint: Showing the top {n} dimension values for high-cardinality fields.
analytics_funnel_subtitle: Pick 2-5 events as steps and a conversion window to see drop-off and conversion rates.
analytics_funnel_empty: No matching conversion paths — check the event names and window.
analytics_funnel_window_too_long: Funnel time range is limited to 31 days.
analytics_activity_subtitle: Raw event stream in reverse chronological order. Filter by any dimension; expand a row for JSON, click trace_id for the full waterfall.
analytics_activity_empty: No matching events in this time range
analytics_activity_open_in_explore: Group by this field in Explore
dashboard_active_trend_subtitle: Split by source: platform signups vs. synced accounts
dashboard_module_health_subtitle: Core metrics per module (click a card to drill in)
dashboard_placeholder_connecting: Placeholder — auto-filled once Tinybird is wired up.
dashboard_tinybird_status_error: Not connected — the server-side Tinybird secrets may be missing.
dashboard_requests_trend_subtitle: Daily API requests and errors over the past 30 days. Source: Tinybird `tenant_request_overview`.
dashboard_requests_empty_subtitle: The `http_requests` datasource is empty. Data will appear seconds after the first API hit.
dashboard_kpi_error_rate_note: % of status>=500 over the past 30 days
storage_box_field_alias_hint: Optional URL-friendly key. Lowercase letters / digits / hyphens / underscores.
storage_box_field_interest_hint: Applied per "period days". e.g. 3% / 365 days = 3% annualized.
storage_box_no_currencies_hint: No currencies yet. Mark an item definition as a currency on the Items page first.
storage_box_delete_description: This will cascade-delete all user deposits and logs and cannot be undone.
storage_box_deposit_no_active: This user has no active deposits.
storage_box_deposit_lookup_hint: Enter an endUserId to look up all of their deposits.
storage_box_deposit_early_blocked: Fixed deposit not yet matured and the config disallows early withdrawal
shop_field_link_activity_hint: When linked, this product is only visible inside that activity's nodes. Leave empty for an always-on shop product.
task_field_filter_hint: Optional. Filters event data — only events whose expression evaluates to true count toward progress.
task_field_filter_syntax_hint: Uses filtrex syntax (==, !=, and, or, not, in, x >= y, x of obj). Use dots for nested fields, e.g. stats.level. Strings in double quotes.
task_field_activity_hint: When linked, this task is auto-included in the activity's task pool. Leave empty for an always-on task.
task_field_reward_tiers_hint: Each tier settles independently once its threshold is reached (autoClaim tasks send rewards via mail, otherwise users claim manually). alias must be unique; threshold must strictly increase and not exceed targetValue.
task_reward_tiers_parse_failed: Failed to parse reward tiers JSON: {error}
task_field_terminal_rewards_hint: Granted when the task fully completes (targetValue is reached).
lottery_field_activity_hint: When linked, this lottery pool is only available inside that activity's nodes. Leave empty for an always-on pool.
check_in_field_activity_hint: Pick an activity to attach this check-in as an activity node. Leave empty for an always-on check-in.
banner_field_activity_hint: When linked, this banner only shows inside that activity's nodes. Leave empty for an always-on slot.
leaderboard_no_entries: No entries in the current period
leaderboard_field_metric_key_hint: Other modules call contribute() against this key; one key can feed multiple configs.
leaderboard_field_reward_tiers_hint: On cycle close, rewards are granted by rank ranges via idempotent mail delivery.
activity_lifecycle_invariant: Must hold: visibleAt ≤ startAt < endAt ≤ hiddenAt
activity_lifecycle_locked_warning: Time fields are locked because this activity has been published and is past the draft phase. Unpublish first to edit times.
activity_countdown_idle: Activity is in draft or already archived — no countdown.
activity_section_rewards_desc: Milestone tiers and global rewards use the visual editor; activity-scoped currencies are defined in the Currency module with activity_id linkage.
activity_global_rewards_hint: Granted once when the activity ends. Use the picker to select items, currencies, or entity blueprints.
activity_node_create_description: One step: create the underlying config → mount it as an activity node. activityId is filled in automatically.
activity_node_field_alias_help: Node alias is unique within this activity; if the resource alias is left empty, this value will be reused.
activity_node_custom_description: A custom node has no underlying resource (refId is null). Useful as a placeholder or for purely client-side logic.
activity_node_entity_schema_help: A blueprint must belong to an entity schema; selecting one renders its specific fields below.
activity_node_alias_required: Please fill in the node alias first
activity_tick_success: tick done: advanced {advanced}, schedules fired {scheduleFired}, errors {errors}
activity_create_success: Activity created (status: draft). Publish from the detail page.
activity_detail_publish_success: Published — state will advance automatically over time
activity_detail_delete_confirm: Delete activity "{name}"? All associated data will be cascade-removed: members, point logs, milestone reward records, schedules, and per-cycle catalog rows (currencies / item definitions / entity blueprints) bound to this activity along with their wallet / inventory rows. Permanent and cannot be undone.
activity_node_alias_unified_hint: Alias is read from the body form below — fill it once. The same value is used as both the resource alias and the activity node alias.
activity_nodes_edit_tooltip: Edit node-level fields (alias, refId, nodeType are immutable)
activity_node_edit_description: Adjust node-level fields. Resource-level edits open in the original module.
activity_node_edit_unlock_rule_hint: Optional. Combine: requirePrevNodeAliases / minActivityPoints / notBefore / relativeToStartSeconds.
activity_node_unlock_prev_nodes_hint: Type a node alias and press Enter (or comma) to add. Player must have claimed those node-level rewards before this node unlocks.
activity_node_unlock_not_before_hint: Player can't access this node until this wall-clock time, regardless of where they are in the activity timeline.
activity_node_unlock_relative_hint: Wait this many seconds after the activity startAt before this node unlocks. Useful for staged rollouts within an activity.
activity_detail_load_failed_desc: The activity may have been removed, or the network is flaky. Try going back to the list.
activity_node_edit_node_config_hint: Inline config for virtual nodes (game_board / custom). Most node types ignore this.
activity_section_cleanup_desc: What happens to player-held activity currencies / temp items after the activity ends
activity_users_search_title: Look up a player by endUserId for the aggregated view
activity_users_search_hint: Enter the SaaS tenant's business user id. We'll fetch progress / points / claimed milestones / per-node unlock state in one round-trip.
activity_nodes_field_ref_id_empty: No global resources of this type
activity_nodes_two_layer_node_desc: Per-activity toggle. Hides only this mount; the underlying resource is untouched.
activity_nodes_two_layer_resource_desc: Global toggle on the underlying resource. Switch from the resource's own edit page.
activity_nodes_two_layer_visible_desc: Node enabled AND resource status = true. Hidden if either is off.
activity_nodes_switch_tooltip: Node-level toggle: controls whether this activity node renders. Does not change the underlying resource.
activity_nodes_resource_tooltip: Status of the underlying resource (resource.isActive). Switch from the resource's own edit page.
activity_nodes_visibility_tooltip_intro: effectiveEnabled = Node enabled AND Resource active.
activity_nodes_delete_tooltip: Removes the mount only — the underlying resource stays in its module.
activity_schedules_cron_hint_suffix: . Stops automatically once the activity is archived.
activity_template_blueprints_currencies_: Each cycle spawns fresh currency rows with `activity_id = <new instance>`. Player wallets are isolated per cycle.
activity_template_blueprints_items_hint: Each cycle spawns fresh item definitions. Player inventories isolated per cycle.
activity_template_blueprints_entities_hi: Each cycle spawns fresh entity blueprints under the chosen schema. `schemaAlias` resolves to a permanent entity_schemas row.
activity_template_blueprints_empty: No rows yet — leave empty to skip.
activity_template_create_alias_pattern_h: Supports {placeholders} placeholders; the expanded alias must be unique within the project.
activity_template_create_field_payload: Template payload (fields copied to each new activity, JSON)
activity_template_create_payload_hint: Supported fields: name / description / kind / timezone / currency / milestoneTiers / globalRewards / kindMetadata / cleanupRule / joinRequirement / visibility / themeColor / bannerImage / metadata
activity_template_create_nodes_hint_intr: Each cycle auto-creates nodes per this template.
activity_template_create_schedules_hint: Seconds offset relative to the new cycle's startAt (used by once_at). Other fields follow the schedules table semantics.
activity_template_create_auto_publish_hi: When enabled, each cycle enters the timed state automatically — no manual publish needed.
activity_form_membership_hint: Optional. With queue enabled, /join assigns a unique random number per activity that admins can redeem.
command_palette_description: Type to search for a command.
rank_delete_config_desc: This permanently removes the tier config and all its tiers. Active seasons referencing it will block deletion.
rank_config_alias_help: Lowercase, letters / digits / -_. Unique per project.
rank_config_base_k_help: Elo sensitivity. 32 is a typical default.
rank_config_perf_weight_help: 0-1, extra delta from performanceScore. 0 disables it.
rank_tier_max_score_help: Leave empty for the top tier.
rank_no_configs: No tier configs yet. Click "New Config" to get started.
rank_season_finalize_desc: This freezes the season, writes the ranking snapshot, and archives the leaderboard config. Cannot be undone.
rank_no_players: No players in this season yet.
rank_no_matches: No matches settled in this season yet.
end_user_subtitle: Users of your game — managed here or synced in from your own system.
end_user_empty_description: Users appear here after they sign up via your game, or after you sync them in.
end_user_detail_sign_out_all_confirm_des: The user will be forced to sign in again on every device.
end_user_detail_disable_confirm_descript: They will be signed out everywhere immediately, and blocked from signing in until you re-enable them.
end_user_detail_delete_confirm_descripti: This removes the account, all sessions, and the password. Business data keyed by endUserId (leaderboards, inventory) is NOT deleted. This cannot be undone.
end_user_detail_toast_disabled: User disabled and all sessions revoked.
end_user_detail_not_found_description: This user may have been deleted or belongs to a different project.
character_empty: No characters yet — create one from the top right.
character_field_alias_hint: Unique per project. Useful for cross-project script references. Leave blank if unsure.
character_field_avatar_hint: Shown next to dialogue bubbles. Square crop recommended.
character_field_portrait_hint: Optional full-body / half-body image for close-ups.
character_delete_desc: Dialogue nodes that reference this character will render as "character:<id>" and cannot be recovered.
dialogue_node_speaker_no_characters_tip: No characters yet? Create one under "Characters" first.
badge_delete_desc: All descendant nodes will also be soft-deleted.
badge_step1_title: Step 1 · Where does this badge appear?
badge_step1_hint: Pick the UI location (key + parent) and the visual style.
badge_step2_title: Step 2 · When does it light up?
badge_step2_hint: Bind the node to a signal, or aggregate from children.
badge_step3_title: Step 3 · When does it go dark?
badge_step3_hint: Choose the dismiss rule. `auto` lets business state decide.
badge_field_key_hint: Dot notation, e.g. home.mail.inbox. Cannot be changed after creation.
badge_field_signal_key_prefix_hint: All signals starting with this prefix are aggregated. Usually ends with '.' — e.g. mail.inbox.
badge_field_dismiss_mode_hint: auto = business state; manual = tap to clear; version = relight on new version; daily = reset at day rollover; session = reset on login; cooldown = tap hides for N seconds.
badge_field_visibility_rule_hint: Optional gate. See placeholder for shape. Node stays hidden when the player context doesn't match.
badge_inspector_subtitle: Debug why a red dot lights up or stays dark for a specific player.
badge_inspector_no_nodes: No nodes (or all hidden by visibility rules).
badge_inspector_no_signals: This player has no matching signals.
badge_inspector_no_dismissals: This player has no dismissal records.
settings_account_description: Manage your profile, email, password, and active sessions.
cms_type_list_empty: No CMS types yet. Create one to define a content schema.
cms_type_alias_hint: Lowercase letters, digits, dash and underscore. Used in URLs and as the API path.
cms_type_group_options_hint: Optional. Comma-separated whitelist that constrains entry.groupKey.
cms_type_delete_confirm_desc: All entries of this type will also be deleted. This cannot be undone.
cms_section_schema_hint: Define what fields entries of this type have.
cms_schema_empty_hint: No fields yet — add one to start.
cms_schema_evolution_warning: Schema is in additive-only mode. You can add new fields, but removing fields, changing a field's type, or making an optional field required is blocked. Copy to a new type for breaking changes.
cms_entry_list_empty: No entries match the current filter.
cms_entry_no_fields: This type has no fields. Edit the schema first.
cms_entry_schema_drift: This entry was saved against schema v{entryVersion}. The current schema is v{typeVersion}. Re-save to upgrade.
activity_phase_draft_tooltip: Draft — invisible to players. Must be explicitly published before progressing.
activity_phase_scheduled_tooltip: Published but before the public-visible time (now < visibleAt). Still invisible to players.
activity_phase_teasing_tooltip: Teaser period — players can see the entry/banner but all participation/write actions (check-in, lottery pull, purchase, score submit, etc.) are rejected.
activity_phase_active_tooltip: Active — players can participate and claim rewards.
activity_phase_ended_tooltip: Ended — players can see the final results (e.g. final leaderboard) but can neither participate nor claim.
activity_phase_archived_tooltip: Archived — invisible to players. Data may be swept by cleanup_rule.
activity_lifecycle_simple_description: Just pick a start and end time. visibleAt defaults to no teasing period, rewardEnd defaults to start+7d, hiddenAt defaults to rewardEnd+30d. Switch to advanced mode to customize.
activity_lifecycle_simple_hint: Advanced mode unlocks all 5 time points: visible / start / end / reward end / hidden.
offline_checkin_description: Geo-anchored check-in campaigns for in-person events — comic cons, designer-toy expos, brand pop-ups, scavenger hunts.
offline_checkin_mode_hint_collect: Players collect stamps from spots in any order.
offline_checkin_mode_hint_daily: Players check in once per day at any spot to accumulate days.
offline_checkin_collection_album_hint: Optional — link a Collection album so spot rewards auto-unlock its entries via the existing collection.onItemGranted hook.
offline_checkin_completion_rewards_hint: Granted once when the completion rule is satisfied. Stamp / album milestone rewards live in the linked Collection.
offline_checkin_no_spots: No spots yet — add the first one below.
offline_checkin_spot_alias_hint: Unique within the campaign — used in QR codes and printed materials.
offline_checkin_spot_radius_hint: How far from the spot a player can be and still pass GPS verification. Add device accuracy on top automatically.
offline_checkin_spot_rewards_hint: Granted on first successful check-in. Items here trigger Collection unlocks via triggerItemDefinitionId.
offline_checkin_spot_collection_aliases_: Comma-separated list of collection_entry aliases this spot maps to (display only; unlocking goes through item rewards).
offline_checkin_verification_no_methods: Choose at least one verification method.
offline_checkin_manual_code_hint: Show this code to the player at the booth. Tap rotate to issue a new code; previous code is invalidated.
experiment_description: Create A/B tests, allocate traffic, compare variant performance
experiment_empty_description: Create your first experiment to start data-driven iteration
experiment_field_key_helper: Stable identifier used in client code. Lowercase, digits, underscore. Cannot be changed later.
experiment_field_control_variant_helper: Fallback variant when a user is not assigned. Usually `control`.
experiment_variants_section_hint: Each variant is one experience. Traffic percentages must sum to 100%.
experiment_variants_empty: No variants yet — create at least 2 (including control)
experiment_variant_field_config_helper: Leave empty for pure traffic split. Fill JSON to deliver per-variant config (reward multipliers, copy, prices).
experiment_variant_delete_confirm_body: This cannot be undone and will be removed from traffic allocation. If users are already assigned to this variant, archive the experiment first.
experiment_traffic_locked_running: Traffic allocation is locked while running. Pause the experiment first.
experiment_running_banner: Experiment is running. Pause first to edit variants or traffic.
experiment_preflight_description: After starting, new users will be assigned variants and existing assignments stay. Confirm all checks pass below.
experiment_preflight_check_variants_hint: Add more in the «Variants & traffic allocation» section of the config tab.
experiment_preflight_check_traffic_hint: Current sum: {sum}%. Adjust in the variants table.
experiment_preflight_check_traffic_orpha: These variant_keys aren't in the variants list: {keys}
experiment_preflight_check_control: Control variant exists and is in the allocation
experiment_preflight_check_control_hint: Need a variant with key "{key}" assigned a non-zero traffic share.
experiment_preflight_confirm_text: I have reviewed the experiment design
experiment_pause_body: New users won't be assigned. {count} already-assigned users keep their variant. Resume anytime.
experiment_archive_body: Archiving stops new assignments and hides this from default lists (still visible under the archived tab). The {users} assigned users' data is preserved.
experiment_delete_confirm_body: Only draft / archived experiments can be deleted. This cannot be undone — variants and assignments are removed too.
experiment_bucketing_section_hint: Type an endUserId to see its variant, or sample 1000 synthetic users to verify the distribution.
experiment_chart_exposure_subtitle: First-exposure user counts split by variant
experiment_chart_no_exposures_yet: No exposures yet — visible ~1 min after start
experiment_chart_funnel_link_hint: Need a variant × business-event funnel comparison? Jump to self-serve analytics with the filter pre-filled.
experiment_chart_draft_hint: Experiment is in draft. No exposure data until you start it.
experiment_error_key_conflict: This key is already in use
experiment_error_not_running: Experiment is not running — cannot assign
experiment_error_variant_in_use: This variant has {count} users assigned. Archive the experiment first.
experiment_targeting_section_hint: Define which users get assigned. Non-matching users are not assigned and not counted as exposures.
experiment_targeting_match_all_hint: Turn off to filter by attribute conditions
experiment_targeting_empty_hint: No conditions yet — add one, e.g. "country is one of [JP, KR]"
experiment_targeting_unsupported_title: This rule uses an advanced JSONLogic expression
experiment_targeting_unsupported_body: The Builder UI doesn't yet support nested AND/OR groups or custom operators. Edit this rule via the API.
experiment_targeting_preview_subtitle: Test a sample attribute set against 1000 synthetic users to see what fraction matches your rule
experiment_targeting_preview_result: ~{rate}% of users would match this rule
experiment_targeting_preview_no_rule: No rule configured — every user enters
experiment_targeting_preview_empty_hint: No targeting rule. Every user will be assigned.
experiment_decision_panel_subtitle: Computes conversion rate, confidence interval, and p-value across control vs variants.
experiment_decision_panel_pick_metric_fi: Pick a conversion event above to start the analysis
experiment_decision_small_sample_note: Total sample below {threshold} — p-values hidden because significance calls are unreliable at small N.
experiment_metric_denominator_users_hint: Standard conversion rate: converted_users / exposed_users. Recommended for z-test.
experiment_metric_denominator_events_hin: Per-user event count: total_events / exposed_users. Use for "actions per user" metrics.
experiment_srm_body: Observed split deviates significantly from configured traffic_allocation (χ²={chi}, p={p}). This usually indicates a bug in bucketing / SDK-side reporting — **all conclusions from this experiment should not be trusted right now.**
experiment_srm_action: Investigate immediately: check recent deploys, SDK versions, and any intermediate redirects that could drop one side's exposures.
experiment_about_stats_subtitle: Three commonly-used statistical concepts in plain language
about_stats_pvalue_lead: p-value is "the probability the observed difference is just random luck." p < 0.05 is the conventional threshold for "this is real, not noise."
about_stats_pvalue_coin: Analogy: 6 heads in 10 coin flips — meh, fair coins do that often. 6000 heads in 10000 flips — that coin is almost certainly biased. Same 60% rate, but bigger sample → smaller p-value.
about_stats_pvalue_threshold: We use 0.05 as the default threshold. When total sample is below 1000, this panel hides p-values — "significant" calls at small N are unstable.
about_stats_ci_lead: More intuitive than a single p-value: "conversion rate is 12%" is a point estimate; the true rate is probably in [10%, 14%]. The 95% CI tells you that range.
about_stats_ci_example: If control's CI is [10%, 14%] and variant's CI is [13%, 17%], the intervals overlap → the gap isn't certain. Fully separated intervals → the gap is likely real.
about_stats_srm_lead: You set 50/50 traffic but the observed split is 55/45 — this kind of imbalance auto-alerts. It usually means a bug in bucketing / reporting (bias in the hash function, SDK dropping exposures on one variant after a redirect, etc).
about_stats_srm_action: When SRM fires, **none of the conversion comparisons can be trusted** — stop analyzing, fix the infrastructure, and restart with fresh data.
about_stats_sample_lead: How much sample you need depends on the smallest lift you want to detect. Detecting +1pp typically needs tens of thousands; +5pp needs only thousands. Low-traffic experiments run for a week often won't yield trustworthy conclusions — either route more traffic, wait longer, or accept that this experiment can't be measured cleanly.
about_stats_footer_disclaimer: This guide is for operators and PMs — it doesn't replace a statistics textbook. For advanced methods (sequential testing, CUPED, Bayesian) consult your data team.
org_switcher_no_projects: No projects in this organization yet
org_switcher_error_not_ready: Organization not ready, please try again
ai_assist_backfill_toast: Form filled — please review before saving
ai_assist_reject_message: That's not quite right, please suggest a different approach
ai_assist_hint_global: Ask questions, check configs, or @-mention resources to let AI suggest changes (you review before saving).
ai_assist_hint_form: Describe what you need, AI will help fill the form. Review before saving.
ai_assist_hint_list: Ask about configs, module descriptions, and more.
ai_assist_suggestion_list_2: Find configs with alias containing daily
global_assist_fab_description: Ask questions, check configs, or @-mention resources to let AI suggest changes (you review before saving). For form-filling, go to the relevant module.
landing_hero_badge: Universal game backend + AI Copilot — one SDK to rule them all
landing_hero_copilot_badge: Built-in AI Copilot · covers {n} business modules
landing_hero_desc: ApolloKit pre-builds {count}+ universal game modules (items, currency, check-in, lottery, events, mail, leaderboards, guilds, dialogue…). New projects plug in via SDK — no more reinventing the wheel.
landing_capabilities_eyebrow: One SDK, all your generic logic
landing_capabilities_title: Six systems — every common game backend abstracted into plug-and-play modules.
landing_cap_economy_desc: Unified resource ledger: items, currency, entities, and inventory all connected. Rewards, deductions, exchanges, and transaction logs in a single call.
landing_cap_liveops_desc: Check-in, banners, announcements, events, lottery, tasks. Scheduling, segmentation, time-limits, combo rewards — rules ready to configure and ship.
landing_cap_content_desc: Story dialogue, encyclopedias, levels, and asset storage are first-class citizens. Multi-language, versioning, and publish workflows in one pipeline.
landing_cap_social_desc: Friends, guilds, teams, leaderboards, ranked ladder — relationship graphs and season settlements built into the platform, no custom code needed.
landing_cap_analytics_desc: Economy flows and event triggers auto-ingested. Second-level funnels, retention, and re-engagement curves from day one — no BI setup required.
landing_cap_sdk_desc: One API Key, one integration. The client calls it; the platform handles rewards, deductions, and logs automatically — no custom services to build.
landing_modules_title_line1: Every module you thought would take 3 months to build
landing_modules_title_line2: is a single toggle in ApolloKit.
landing_module_economy_subtitle: Economy · Items, currency, trading, inventory in one schema
landing_module_ops_subtitle: LiveOps · Events, check-in, lottery ready to configure
landing_module_content_subtitle: Content · Let designers publish content themselves
landing_module_social_subtitle: Social · Let players retain each other
landing_workflow_eyebrow: From new project to game launch
landing_workflow_title: 4 steps to give your new game a complete backend.
landing_workflow_step1_body: Open a project in the console, get your API Key and dashboard. The whole process takes under 1 minute.
landing_workflow_step2_body: Select the modules your game needs — items, check-in, leaderboard, mail… Configure rules, rewards, and cycles in the console.
landing_workflow_step3_body: Integrate the ApolloKit SDK on the client. Granting rewards, deductions, claiming mail, and lotteries are all single function calls with type-safe parameters.
landing_workflow_step4_body: Hand the generic backend logic to ApolloKit and let your team focus on gameplay, art, and story.
landing_copilot_desc: ApolloKit Copilot is embedded in every console page — it understands the event, lottery, or mail you're editing, can fill fields directly, carry context across pages, and look up docs. Operators don't need to memorize 14 rule sets; one sentence is enough.
landing_copilot_feat_coverage_desc: Events, check-in, mail, shop, lottery… Copilot knows them all — it's not just a chat box.
landing_copilot_feat_autofill_desc: Understands which page you're editing and auto-breaks "Double 11 Weekend Double Check-in" into individual fields.
landing_copilot_feat_persist_desc: Jump from events to mail — conversation stays; drag the sidebar anywhere and keep AI in view.
landing_copilot_feat_docs_desc: Summon the docs site at any time, get streaming Markdown answers with auto-linked references.
landing_copilot_mock_user_message: "Help me set up Double 11 Weekend Double Check-in"
landing_copilot_mock_ai_detail: ↳ 3 fields filled · referenced docs §4.2 lottery probability
landing_analytics_desc: ApolloKit has a built-in real-time analytics engine — rewards, lotteries, and event triggers are auto-ingested. Funnels, retention, and re-engagement curves are available from launch day. Operators review their own data without waiting on the data team.
landing_analytics_feat_ingest_desc: All 30+ modules auto-feed the real-time analytics engine. Zero tagging, zero ETL — new modules are captured on launch.
landing_analytics_feat_funnel_desc: D1 / D7 / D30 retention calculated at second level; adjust an event parameter and see the curve change instantly.
landing_analytics_feat_ab_desc: Pull data and check results the same day you launch. Slice by group, segment, payment tier — any combination.
landing_analytics_feat_api_desc: Pipe Token outputs JSON / CSV directly; plug into BI, dashboards, or Slack bots.
landing_dev_title: One API Key, one type-safe SDK.
landing_dev_desc: Every module has a corresponding SDK method, auto-synced to your TypeScript / C# / Go project. Rewards, deductions, mail claims, and lotteries are all single function calls — the compiler tells you immediately if you get a parameter wrong.
landing_dev_feat_sdk_desc: TypeScript / C# / Go / Python — one interface, always in sync
landing_dev_feat_apikey_desc: Per-environment, per-module scopes with fine-grained permissions
landing_dev_feat_events_desc: Credits, debits, event triggers pushed to any of your services
landing_dev_feat_global_desc: Player requests served from the closest node, naturally low latency
landing_dev_code_comment: // Send a system mail with reward to a player
landing_events_desc: Player reward claims, event triggers, lottery settlements… ApolloKit abstracts them into a unified event stream. Async queue fan-out never blocks the main request; HMAC-signed Webhooks push to your services; the Trigger engine lets events chain together.
landing_events_target_trigger_sub: Events trigger events · closed loop on platform
landing_events_webhook_comment: // Platform push · HMAC signature · auto-retry on failure
landing_stack_title: The dirty work underneath — you never have to touch it.
landing_stack_desc: ApolloKit is not another backend you have to maintain yourself. Servers, databases, identity, permissions, scaling, disaster recovery — all handled by the platform. You focus on gameplay, art, and story — the things that actually make players stay.
landing_stack_global_desc: Services auto-distributed to the node nearest each player — Tokyo, Frankfurt, São Paulo all equally stable.
landing_stack_serverless_desc: No machines to scale, no databases to tune. Platform handles capacity and availability.
landing_stack_sdk_desc: TypeScript / C# / Go multi-language SDK. The compiler tells you when a parameter is wrong.
landing_stack_auth_desc: Account system, project collaboration, Dev/Staging/Prod-isolated scope API Keys — one interface for three environments.
landing_stack_ai_desc: Built-in document RAG. Operations Copilot and docs Ask-AI out of the box — no model integration needed.
landing_stack_audit_desc: Every resource change, every event trigger is recorded. Customer complaints and audits answered in seconds.
landing_pricing_title: Prototype with 500 MAU free forever; upgrade when you launch commercially.
landing_pricing_desc: Hybrid pricing by monthly active players + feature tier — not DAU. Traffic spikes from version updates, new server launches, and holiday leaderboards won't spike your bill.
landing_cta_h2_line1: Stop letting your next game's backend
landing_cta_desc: ApolloKit is free to start. Register and instantly get a console + API Key — integrating a new project takes just one afternoon.
pricing_studio_feat4: Ticket support · 1 business day first response
pricing_enterprise_feat7: Annual security audit & penetration test report
pricing_matrix_label_liveops: LiveOps (check-in / banner / announcement / event / lottery / task)
pricing_faq_q1: Why charge by MAU, not DAU?
pricing_faq_a1: Game DAU fluctuates more than you'd expect — version launch day, new server opens, holiday events, ranking pushes, all can multiply DAU several times. Charging by DAU means your bill multiplies too. MAU pricing naturally smooths those spikes over the month, making budgets easier to estimate.
pricing_faq_a2: Within a calendar month, any account identified by userId that has at least one activity (login, claim reward, gacha, upload match, etc.) counts as 1 MAU. Repeated activity is not counted twice. Dev/test environment projects don't count toward production quota.
pricing_faq_q3: What happens if I exceed my quota?
pricing_faq_a3: Service won't cut off immediately. We notify in advance, suggest an upgrade, and allow short-term overage (15-day surge window covering launch / new server / holiday spikes). Overage is billed at a per-1000-MAU add-on fee — no outage from delayed tier approval.
pricing_faq_a4: Yes. Studio and above get a 20% discount on annual billing, plus dedicated migration assistance.
pricing_faq_q5: What are the Indie free tier limits?
pricing_faq_a5: 500 MAU/month, 1 project, 2 members; all features available except SSO / Webhook / custom domain / SLA. Indie is for prototype validation, internal testing, and student projects — once you're ready to go commercial, upgrade to Studio.
pricing_faq_q6: Why only 500 MAU free, not more?
pricing_faq_a6: Games aren't tools — a game with 1,000+ stable MAU typically has commercial revenue. We set the free tier at 500 MAU so demos, betas, and campus projects can run completely, while avoiding subsidizing commercial projects with revenue on a free plan.
pricing_faq_q7: Can I upgrade from Indie to Studio without losing data?
pricing_faq_a7: Yes. All data, configurations, and API Keys carry over within your account. Upgrading only unlocks more quota and capabilities — no migration needed.
pricing_faq_a8: Single-tenant deployment (dedicated infrastructure) and region-dedicated deployment are supported. For fully offline self-hosted solutions, OEM contracts with images and support are available.
pricing_faq_q9: Are there special plans for students / open source / non-commercial projects?
pricing_faq_a9: Educational, open source, and non-commercial mod projects can apply for a free Studio upgrade (subject to review). Contact sales@apollokit.dev.
pricing_hero_h1_free: Free at the prototype stage, forever.
pricing_hero_desc: Not DAU, not machine count — billed by real monthly actives and the features you need. Up to 500 MAU, Indie is free forever — good for prototypes, beta tests, and thesis projects; commercial launches go Studio. Traffic spikes from updates, new servers, and holiday rankings won't blow your budget.
pricing_matrix_desc: Full feature list at a glance — so your team can decide without guessing "do we have this?"
pricing_faq_title: The questions we get asked most.
pricing_cta_h2: Still on the fence? Start with 500 MAU and see for yourself.
pricing_cta_desc: Zero risk, zero credit card, zero sales calls. When you're ready to launch commercially, upgrading to Studio is just a click away.
